narrative_blocks.header: Directed CDC TB-HIV research program in Botswana
narrative_blocks.body: James spent nine years overseas advising governments on infectious disease — directing the U.S. CDC's tuberculosis and HIV research program in Gaborone, Botswana, advising the Government of India on tuberculosis control, and helping the University of Maryland start a large AIDS treatment program in Nigeria. He returned to Yale as Associate Professor of Internal Medicine and faculty of the Yale Institute for Global Health.

education.school_name: Columbia University College of Physicians and Surgeons

career.organization: U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC)
career.notes: Directed CDC's TB and HIV research program in Botswana for several years; family resident in Gaborone during this period.

career.organization: University of Maryland AIDS treatment program
career.notes: Helped start a large AIDS treatment program in Nigeria.

career.role: Associate Professor of Internal Medicine (Infectious Diseases); Affiliated Faculty, Yale Institute for Global Health
career.notes: Returned to Yale after nine years overseas.

institutions.institution_blurb: Yale School of Medicine — Associate Professor of Internal Medicine (Infectious Diseases); Affiliated Faculty, Yale Institute for Global Health.

parents.mother_research_notes: Mother Katherine 'Kitty' Woodward Mellon (I00329) — Michael's second wife, born ~1937 SF, died 2012 Stonington CT. After divorcing Michael, Kitty married Richard Prosser Mellon (X02365) of Ligonier PA — making Richard Katherine's stepfather (devoted, per Richard's 2020 obituary).

narrative_blocks.body: Katherine carries her great-grandmother's surname 'Shreve' as her own middle name. Elizabeth Lawton Shreve married Robert Gay Hooker and was the matriarch of the California Hooker line that produced both Katherine's grandfather Rodman Lent Hooker and her father Michael.

cross_connections.display_label: Carries great-grandmother's surname Shreve as her middle name

naming_inspiration.description: Middle name Shreve carries forward her great-grandmother Elizabeth Lawton Shreve, who married Robert Gay Hooker (H04266) and was the mother of Michael's father Rodman Lent Hooker (HD3246).

parents.mother_research_notes: Mother Nancy Borland Morse, daughter of Samuel F.B. Morse 'The Boss' (Pebble Beach developer).

marriages.notes: Wedding at the Episcopal Church of St. Matthew; reception at Burlingame Country Club.

narrative_blocks.header: Hole-in-one on the 15th at Cypress Point
narrative_blocks.body: Among the four career holes-in-one Michael notched as a passionate lifelong golfer was one on the famous 15th hole at Cypress Point Club on the Monterey Peninsula. The course is among the most exclusive in American golf, and the 15th — a downhill par-3 against the Pacific cliffs — sits in the most photographed stretch of the game.

narrative_blocks.header: Marine Corps pilot flying jets off Pacific carriers
narrative_blocks.body: After realizing accounting at Penn wasn't for him, Michael joined the US Marine Corps as a pilot. He earned his wings at Pensacola and spent his service flying jets off carriers across the Pacific — a long way from his Hillsborough and Pebble Beach upbringing alongside his maternal grandfather Samuel Finley Brown Morse, the Pebble Beach developer known to family as 'The Boss'.

narrative_blocks.header: The Hook of Burlingame, Pacific Union, Cypress Point
narrative_blocks.body: Friends called him 'the Hook,' and his San Francisco peninsula life ran through three of the region's most exclusive clubs — Burlingame Country Club where he and Connie celebrated their wedding reception in 1995, the Pacific Union Club in San Francisco, and Cypress Point Club on the Monterey Peninsula where he hit one of his four career holes-in-one. Outside the clubs, fly fishing on Henry's Fork of the Snake River was the constant — Michael regularly depleted the trout stock there.

cross_connections.display_label: Carried four-greats-grandfather Bunker's Gay name into the twentieth century

education.notes: Concord, New Hampshire. Active in hockey, football, and track.
Attended but did not graduate — 'realizing accounting wasn't a passion' per his obituary, he left to enlist as a Marine pilot.

career.notes: From small start-ups to larger firms; built lifelong relationships with institutional clients.

military_service.notes: Basic training in San Diego; received aviator's wings at Pensacola. Flew jets off carriers across the Pacific. Post-Korea, pre-Vietnam era.

birth.note: Year derived from age 74 at March 2012 death.

parents.father_research_notes: Father Robert Latham Woodward of San Francisco (per 2012 obituary). Not built into tree.
parents.mother_research_notes: Mother referred to as 'Mrs. Robert Latham Woodward'.

marriages.notes: Her second marriage; Richard's second. Through this marriage Katherine Hooker Shepherd (HD3388) gained Richard P. Mellon as a stepfather.

narrative_blocks.header: Sat on Courtauld Institute and Art Services boards
narrative_blocks.body: Kitty served as a board member of Art Services International — the Washington-based travelling-exhibition organization — and the American Friends of the Courtauld Institute, the U.S. support group for the London art history institute. Her San Francisco art-collecting and museum life ran in parallel to her Mellon-family marriage in Pennsylvania.

Founded 1810 as the Medical Institution of Yale College, the fourth oldest medical school in the United States.
